content.rule: Before writing any code, design, or copy, the executing agent stops and evaluates whether they really have the expertise to deliver with quality. If the answer is no, fire the Hiring Loop or escalation before failing, not after.
content.why: Claude knows almost everything superficially. An Engineer Manager told to implement APNs will try — because it has superficial knowledge from training. That's exactly the failure mode. A rule saying 'ask for help when you don't know' is not enough because the model will think it knows. A mechanism that forces meta-reasoning to be materialized is needed.
how_to_apply: Mechanism 1 (Preventive): fill in the pre-execution check form before every task — specific domain, specialist availability, worst case assessment, confidence level with citable source. Writing it forces honesty
Mechanism 2 (Structural): each Manager maintains a trust gradient table by task category. Some categories NEVER execute without a specialist (crypto/auth/security, native bridging, infra/deploy/CI)

Mechanism 3 (Reactive): when peer review detects failure due to lack of expertise in domain X, Leo adds X to the 'Always specialist' list for that Manager in the current project. Each mistake corrects the system

Mechanism 4 (Reflective): after peer review rejection, before fixing, run lessons learned pass — identify which rule would have prevented the failure, propose where the lesson should live, get R2 from owner
anti_patterns: Claiming high confidence without a citable source (specialist, memory, doc)
Writing 'yes' on the pre-execution check without actually verifying
Attempting a task in a 'Low/Always' trust category without a specialist
Skipping the lessons learned pass after a review rejection
content.responsibility: Every executing agent (Managers when executing, specialists during work). Reviewers also consult the trust gradient when evaluating whether execution was legitimate.

mechanisms.name: Pre-execution check
mechanisms.description: Mandatory form filled before every task: domain, specialist availability, worst case, confidence with source. Must be written, not just thought.

mechanisms.name: Trust gradient
mechanisms.description: Per-Manager table of task categories with default trust levels. Categories marked 'Always' require specialist regardless of scope or pressure.

mechanisms.name: Post-failure hardening
mechanisms.description: When peer review detects expertise gap failure, Leo automatically adds the domain to 'Always specialist' list for that Manager in the project.

mechanisms.name: Lessons learned pass
mechanisms.description: After review rejection, agent identifies which rule would have prevented failure, whether lesson is project-specific or universal, and proposes where it should be added. Goes through R2.
